redaction_conventions: Use [ATHLETE], [ACTIVITY_ID], [EVENT_ID], [WORKOUT_ID], [DATE], and [RACE_DATE] placeholders in committed artifacts.
Use shifted relative windows instead of real dates.
Do not commit raw athlete identifiers, private comments, exact thresholds, body metrics, or API keys.

prompts.prompt: Review my last 14 days of training. Summarize load, intensity, notable workouts, and recovery risk, then recommend what I should do for the next 48 hours. Do not invent unavailable metrics.
Look at my last 21 days of wellness and recent training. Explain sleep, HRV, resting heart rate, fatigue, and whether I appear ready to train today. Keep subjective scale labels separate.
Compare my completed training this week with the next 14 days of planned work. Identify conflicts, missing easy days, and one practical adjustment without changing my calendar.
I have a race on [RACE_DATE]. Review my fitness trend, wellness, and planned sessions around race week, then suggest a taper outline. Do not write or delete events.

Using [ACTIVITY_ID] from my recent activities, explain what happened in that workout: sport, local start time, duration, distance, load, source/device, and any notes without quoting private text verbatim.
For [ACTIVITY_ID], summarize intervals or laps and per-km or per-mile splits in my preferred units. If either data source is unavailable, say so rather than filling in missing values.
What are my recent best efforts and curve signals? Compare short, threshold, and endurance durations where the data exists, and state units clearly.

For [ACTIVITY_ID], tell me which advanced metrics are actually available, such as decoupling, IF, VI, zone distribution, RPE, feel, compliance, or device name. Do not compute fields the server does not expose.
Find a workout-library option or planned event that could fit next week and draft a short non-destructive note I could add. Do not delete anything or overwrite an existing workout.
Triage [ATHLETE] for this week as a coach: recent activities, upcoming planned work, fitness trend, wellness red flags, and one concise coaching note. Be explicit about data you cannot access.
